analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 (counterparty IDs): merged `Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4/trades_round_4_day_{1,2,3}.csv` with `prices_round_4_day_*.csv` on (day,timestamp,symbol). Forward horizon K in {5,20,100} = K steps along the ordered timestamp series per (day,symbol). Aggressor: trade price vs same-tick bid1/ask1 (buy_agg if price>=ask1, sell_agg if price<=bid1). Stratification: symbol, U-spread bin (tight <=6 vs wide), burst detection (>=3 trades same day+timestamp). Graph: buyer→seller counts and 2-hop motifs. Baseline: cell mean fwd_mid_20 by (buyer,seller,symbol); residuals near zero for dominant pairs.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate object appended as `round4_phase1_complete` in this same file (next array element). Ran `trader_v0.py` round 4 backtests (worse+all, PnL 0) for auditable logs. Do not start Phase 2 depth until reviewed; Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300 K=20 means **flip across days**; burst−control extract test **negative** on average.

phase1_bullets.conclusion: Several Marks show **positive mean same-symbol forward mid** after their prints on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** (e.g. Mark 67 `U_buy_agg` K=5 n≈164 mean≈1.96 ticks, t≈13; Mark 22/49 passive-sell roles on U with n≈90–104 and K∈{5,20}). Voucher-specific aggressive rows are smaller n and weaker t-stats; need execution filter before claiming tradeable PnL.

Residuals of fwd_mid_20 after subtracting (buyer,seller,symbol) cell means are **~0** for high-volume pairs (Mark67→Mark22/49 on U, Mark14↔38 hydro/VEV_4000); **no** large stable second-order anomaly in this linear cell baseline.

Hub structure matches `r4_initial_analysis_summary.txt`: **Mark01→Mark22** (1339 prints) and **Mark14↔Mark38** hydro duopoly dominate; 2-hop listing is heuristic but shows Mark01→Mark22→* as frequent scaffold.

Multi-symbol bursts exist (orchestrator table). Matched **random single-trade** control: mean(**burst_fwd_U − control_fwd_U**) is **negative** at K=5 and K=20 (n≈318 bursts) — **no** evidence bursts predict **better** extract short-horizon mids than random timestamps on this crude control.
Population-level forward mids after `buy_agg` vs `sell_agg` pooled across all symbols are small; use per-symbol tables in full CSV for MM design — not a single smoking gun here.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: After **Mark 67** **buyer-aggressive** prints on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT**, pooled forward extract mid rises ~**2 ticks** at K=5 and ~**1.8** at K=20 (n≈164; t≈5.9–13).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: Tape-only; **worse** fills not tested; role is aggressive **buyer** — any live rule must respect limits and adverse selection at touch.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: **Mark 22** as **passive seller** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** rows: positive mean fwd mid at K=5 and K=20 (n=90; t≈10–4.7).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: Passive seller → upward drift may reflect **liquidity provision** context; needs pairing with aggressor identity and gate (Phase 3 Sonic).

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: **Mark 49** passive seller on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** shows similar positive short-horizon fwd (n≈104).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: Smaller actor than Mark22; confirm not duplicate of same micro episodes.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: **Mark 14** passive seller on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** at **K=100** weak positive mean (~1.1 ticks, n=328, t≈2.2).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: Long horizon signal is mild; overlapping windows not de-duplicated.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: Directed pair **Mark01→Mark22** is the structural spine for voucher basket flow (1339 rows) — candidate **scheduler** feature for any conditional model even if mean reversion on VEV_5300 alone is weak.
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: This is **structural** not yet a signed markout edge; per-symbol Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300 K=20 means **flip sign across days** (see stability CSV).

no_edge_or_negative_findings: **Mark01→Mark22** on **VEV_5300** fwd_mid_20: day means **-0.70 / +0.18 / +0.014** (n 32/31/69) — **not stable** across days for a simple fade/trend rule.
**Burst** timestamps do **not** outperform **random** single-trade timestamps on extract forward mid (mean burst−control < 0 for K=5,20).
**(buyer,seller,symbol)** cell-mean baseline for fwd_mid_20 leaves **near-zero** residuals for dominant hydro and U pairs — no obvious mis-modeled bot residual edge at this granularity.
